prompt.task: Create a 3-email nurture sequence for the product launch described below.
prompt.context: This email sequence targets existing newsletter subscribers who have shown interest in productivity tools. The sequence should build anticipation, provide value, and drive conversions without being pushy. Brand voice: Professional but approachable, data-driven, no hype.

constraints.constraint: Each email must be under 200 words (body text only).
Subject lines must be under 50 characters.
Include exactly one clear CTA per email.
No exclamation marks in subject lines.
Each email must provide standalone value even if not clicked.
Space emails 3 days apart; reference timing in the copy.

prompt.input:
Product: TaskFlow Pro - an AI-powered task management app Launch date: March 15 Key features: - Smart priority suggestions based on deadlines and dependencies - Automatic time blocking in calendar - Team workload visualization - Integration with Slack, Notion, Linear
Target audience: Team leads and project managers at tech startups Price: $12/user/month, 14-day free trial Early bird offer: 30% off annual plan for first 500 signups
prompt.output_format:
For each email, provide:
--- ## Email [N]: [Internal Name] **Send:** [Day relative to sequence start] **Subject:** [Subject line] **Preview text:** [First 90 chars shown in inbox]
**Body:** [Email body with {{first_name}} personalization token where appropriate]
**CTA Button:** [Button text] **CTA Link:** [Destination description] ---
After all emails, include: ## Sequence Notes - Key metrics to track - A/B test suggestions - Segment recommendations

example.output: Subject: "How we reduced meeting overload by 40%" NOT: "You won't BELIEVE this productivity hack!"

checks.check: All three emails have distinct angles (not repetitive).
No banned words appear in the copy.
Each email works independently if others aren't opened.
